title: urceola tournieri
intro: Uriceola tournieri, also known as the Tournier's urceola, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating pain, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. Urceola tournieri is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems are used to make herbal teas, which are believed to possess therapeutic properties.
